title: nasa rugosa subsp. pygmaea
intro: Nasa rugosa subsp. pygmaea, also known as the dwarf nasa or pygmaeum nasa, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Loasaceae, native to the Andean region of South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Nasa rugosa subsp. pygmaea include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever and digestive issues.
